title: ATAH Consent Receipt (stored form)
description: ATAH Core Protocol object: the stored form of a consent receipt. Distinct from consent-receipt.schema.json. Contains the receipt hash, the canonical reference metadata, and a non-identifying continuity binding sufficient to validate later consumption against the original consent. The continuity binding (`client_id`, `pseudonymous_consumer_ref` HMAC, and where applicable `data_categories_hash`) prevents receipt replay (Platform X submits a receipt for user A then attempts to consume it for action concerning user B) and cross-user confusion. The full receipt remains with the asserting platform; ATAH does not store consumer-identifying fields or free-text consent in this schema. Privacy-floor enforcement: a Stage 8 build-time test reads this file and rejects the literal field names that the privacy floor (Charter Part One) excludes; the build-time test additionally verifies the continuity-binding fields are HMAC-shaped or hash-shaped, not plaintext. Required for any ATAH-conformant implementation.

receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalised full consent receipt held by the asserting platform.

client_id.description: Continuity binding: the asserting platform's client identifier captured at receipt issuance. Mirrored from the source receipt's `asserted_by.client_application.client_id`. On consumption, ATAH verifies the consuming session's `client_id` matches this value; mismatch is a continuity-binding violation per §4.10.

consent_type.description: Mirrors the consent_type on the full receipt. Engagement consent is excluded by design and not a valid value.

scope.description: The protocol action the stored receipt covers. Mirrors the scope on the full receipt. `query_submission` pairs with `consent_type: query_authorization`; the remaining values pair with `consent_type: disclosure_consent`.

pseudonymous_consumer_ref.description: Continuity binding: HMAC of the asserting platform's `consumer_ref`, computed under a per-context salted HMAC key (per the F1.16 / §11.8 HMAC-not-plain-hash requirement). Required for consumer-tied scopes (`stage_*` and `outcome_reporting`); MAY be absent for `query_submission`. On consumption, ATAH verifies the consuming session's pseudonymous reference matches this value; mismatch is a continuity-binding violation per §4.10.

data_categories_hash.description: Continuity binding: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalised `data_categories` array from the source receipt. Required when `consent_type` is `disclosure_consent`; MAY be absent for `query_authorization` (which does not carry data_categories). On consumption, ATAH verifies the requested data categories match this hash; mismatch is a continuity-binding violation per §4.10.
